video_title: Galaxy S26 Ultra vs iPhone 17 Pro Max vs Galaxy S25 Ultra – Heavy Workload Performance Test!
text: Excellent comparison as always. However, it is worth noting a significant variable regarding the Galaxy S26 Ultra. As a brand-new device, it is still in its 'learning usage patterns' phase. During the first week or two, the system is aggressively indexing files, synchronising accounts, and the AI is calibrating performance profiles based on user behaviour. This background activity typically leads to higher thermal output and accelerated battery drain. Since the S25 Ultra in this test is already fully optimised and 'settled', it holds a distinct advantage in efficiency and stability. A re-test in two weeks would likely yield more accurate results once the S26 Ultra’s background processes have stabilised.

text: @NoxiousNinja I think this is correct. Light perception is logarithmic, so to perceive something twice as bright, you need to quadruple the power. That would suggest that privacy mode actually DRAINS more battery even though you're turning off half the LEDs. The remaining half of the LEDs that are on need to be MORE than twice as bright as they were before to maintain the same level of brightness. But, Samsung probably knows this, and has made the tradeoff that turning on privacy mode slightly DECREASES your brightness, so that overall power consumption is roughly the same, assuming you don't adjust your brightness settings after turning it on.

text: My theory on these color choices: Apple is barely changing their phones every year, and visually they usually look the same. Doing color schemes likes this means that people can more easily differentiate model years, meaning that more pressure will be put on people who have older models to get newer models, and without Apple having to worry about spending extra $ to meaningfully change the models beyond the color. This is how Apple operates recently. These color choices were definitely very strategically thought out, and not with consumer's best interests in mind
